content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 13, 1989

Lorraine Washington
City Clerk
City Hall
P.O. Box 430
Watsonville, CA 95077

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-89-183

Dear Ms. Washington:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice on behalf
of candidates for Watsonville City Council regarding the
candidates' campaign disclosure requirements under the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").1/ The telephone advice was provided by
Alice Hughes, and I am writing to confirm the advice. 2/

The general municipal election for the City of Watsonville,
scheduled for May 9, 1989, was postponed to an unknown future date
pursuant to litigation involving the conduct of the city's
elections. A new election date will be determined as part of a
plan which must be approved by the court with jurisdiction over
the case.

(1) The candidates for the canceled May 9, 1989 election
should file semiannual campaign disclosure statements for the
first six months of 1989 and for each subsequent six month portion
of a calendar year until they cease to be candidates. (Sections
84200 and 82007.) The period covered by the first semiannual
statement is January 1, 1989 through June 30, 1989, and it is due
on or before July 31, 1989. (Section 84200.) Preelection
statements will be required to be filed by the city council

1/ Government Code Sections 8100-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Your letter poses general filing questions. Therefore, we
consider it to be a request for informal assistance pursuant to
Regulation 18329(c) (copy enclosed). Informal assistance does not
provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Section 84113; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

candidates at such times and covering such time periods as is
appropriate to the date of the election as determined by the
outcome of the City's election procedure litigation. (Sections
84200.7 and 84200.8.)

     (2) If a candidate has filed a statement of intention to be
a candidate for a specific city council office at the May 9, 1989
city election (Form 501), has established a single campaign
account for the purpose of expending funds to seek the office
noticed in the statement of intention, and has provided notice to
the Commission of the campaign account (Form 502), then the
candidate may continue to solicit and to receive campaign funds
and may continue to expend these campaign funds for the purpose of
seeking the office as specified in the original Form 501. When
the day for the election to city council is determined, the
candidate must amend his or her Form 501 and Form 502 to disclose
the date of the election.

     If you have further questions, you may call me at (916) 322-
5662.


                                        Sincerely,

                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                              By:  Bruce W. Robeck
                                   Political Reform Consultant

CITY OF WATSONVILLE

March 22, 1989

Mayor &
City Council

250 Main

Administration

215 Union

728-6011

728-6013

728-6005

728-6029

Rehab. Housing
728-6022
231 Union

Fair Political Practices Commission
Alice Hughes
Technical Assistance
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Alice:

This letter will confirm our conversation by telephone
today.

As I explained to you, the May 9, 1989, General Municipal Election was canceled by the City Council on March
21, 1989. This action was taken after the U.S.
Supreme Court denied the City's Petition for Writ of
Certiorari to the U.S. Court of Appeals for the
Ninth Court on March 20, 1989. The City was sued
by MALDEF in 1985 charging the City's at-large voting
system violated the Voting Rights Act of 1965. The
case was heard in the U.S. District Court for the
Northern District of California and a decision in
favor of the City was handed down on January 30, 1987.
However, the decision was appealed by MALDEF and
heard in the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth
Court. That court reversed the decision of the U.S.
District Court in July of 1988. The City then filed
the Writ of Certiorari in December of 1988.

The City must now devise a districting plan to be
implemented as soon as possible. A new election
date will be set when the District Court Judge approves
the plan.

My questions to you this morning were:

(1) Do the candidates for the canceled May election
file the first campaign statement that was due
on March 30, 1989?

Your advice: candidates should file a semi-
annual statement in July for the period from
January 1 to June 30, 1989.

(2) Can the money raised from the time of nomination
to present be used for the election (date un-
certain at this time)?

City Hall, P.O. Box 430, Watsonville, CA 95077

Fair Political Practices Commission
Page 2
March 22, 1989


Your advice: the money raised can be used for
the new election because the election was
canceled.

(3) What about Forms 501 and 502?

Your advice: amended forms should be filed
when candidates again qualify for new elections;
and a letter should accompany the form explain-
ing the canceled election and stating the new
election date.

(4) Can candidates continue to accept contributions?

Your advice: yes, as long as Forms 501 and 502
have been filed.

You also checked your print out and found that the
following candidates have filed Forms 501 and 502:
Todd McCarren, Jeffrey Solinas, Rebecca J. Garcia,
and Wanda "Gwen" Carroll. Three other candidates
were not on your print out, and I will notify them
again of this obligation.

I request that the above information be verified by
letter so I may pass it along to the candidates and
our City Attorney.

As usual, your prompt assistance is very much
appreciated.

Sincerely,

Lorraine Washington
City Clerk

LW:tr

cc: Don Haile
City Attorney

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 29, 1989


Lorraine Washington
City Clerk
City Hall
P.O. Box 430
Watsonville, CA  95077

Re:  Letter No. 89-183

Dear Ms. Washington:

     We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice
under the Political Reform Act on March 27, 1989.  Your letter has
been assigned to our Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
for response.  If you have any questions, you may contact that
division directly at (916) 322-5662.

     If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without further
analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response.  A confirming
response will be released after it has gone through our approval
process.  If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the
staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you
shortly to advise you.  In such cases, the normal analysis, review
and approval process will be followed.

     You should be aware that your letter and our response are
public records which may be disclosed to any interested person
upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Sincerely,

[signature]

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

428 I Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: What are the campaign disclosure filing requirements for candidates in a canceled May 9, 1989 Watsonville City Council election, and how should campaign funds and forms be handled given the postponed election date?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Candidates must file semiannual campaign disclosure statements by July 31, 1989 for the first half of 1989, followed by preelection statements at appropriate times once the new election date is determined. Candidates who have properly filed Forms 501 and 502 may continue to solicit, receive, and expend campaign funds for the originally noticed office, but must amend both forms when the new election date is established.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This document contains multiple letters: an incoming request from City Clerk Lorraine Washington (March 22, 1989), an acknowledgment letter from FPPC (March 29, 1989), and the final advice letter from FPPC General Counsel (April 13, 1989). The advice letter is the primary FPPC response. Document marked as informal advice per Regulation 18329(c). OCR quality appears good with minor formatting inconsistencies.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Do the candidates for the canceled May election file the first campaign statement that was due on March 30, 1989? Can the money raised from the time of nomination be used for the election (date uncertain at this point)? What about Forms 501 and 502? Can candidates continue to accept contributions?

CONCLUSION: (1) The candidates for the canceled May 9, 1989 election should file semiannual campaign disclosure statements for the first six months of 1989 and for each subsequent six month portion of a calendar year until they cease to be candidates. The period covered by the first semiannual statement is January 1, 1989 through June 30, 1989, and it is due on or before July 31, 1989. Preelection statements will be required to be filed by the city council candidates at such times and covering such time periods as is appropriate to the date of the election as determined by the outcome of the City's election procedure litigation. (2) If a candidate has filed a statement of intention to be a candidate for a specific city council office at the May 9, 1989 city election (Form 501), has established a single campaign account for the purpose of expending funds to seek the office noticed in the statement of intention, and has provided notice to the Commission of the campaign account (Form 502), then the candidate may continue to solicit and to receive campaign funds and may continue to expend these campaign funds for the purpose of seeking the office as specified in the original Form 501. When the day for the election to city council is determined, the candidate must amend his or her Form 501 and Form 502 to disclose the date of the election.

embedding.summary: This FPPC advice letter confirms telephone counsel regarding campaign disclosure requirements for candidates in the canceled May 9, 1989 Watsonville City Council election, which was postponed due to voting rights litigation. The Commission advises on semiannual filing requirements, campaign fund use, and form amendments in light of the postponed election.
